meaning: n.用具,配备,机构,全套装配,vt.配备,装备vi.得到装备
examples: She was wearing an outfit she'd bought the previous day.
Her stage outfit of hot trousers, over-the-knee boots and a tube top was a bit much.
The revamp includes replacing the old navy uniform with a crisp blue and white cotton outfit.
Typical among unhappy customers is Diana Stever, owner of an eight-employee truck repair outfit in Duenweg, Mo.
In the late 1960s Allen-Bradley bought a small outfit that made programmable logic controllers--custom minicomputers.
